case_name: JEAN BENNETT, Personal Representative v. MICHAEL COLLINS

summary.brief: JEAN BENNETT, Personal Representative v. MICHAEL COLLINS. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: This lawsuit involves the decedent's treatment for a shoulder injury beginning on July 6, 2020, until his death on August 8, 2020, with Collins attending to him from August 4 to August 5, 2020.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On July 6, the decedent, a fifty-eight year old man, presented to the emergency department at Cape Cod Hospital (CCH or hospital) via ambulance because he was unable to use his left arm.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Four days later, on July 10, the decedent returned to CCH's emergency department due to increased and worsening pain in his left hand and shoulder, with the antibiotic having failed to improve his condition.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On the next day, August 4, at 9:42 P.M., the decedent again went to CCH's emergency department by ambulance, this time with complaints of general weakness that had lasted for sixteen hours, left shoulder pain after chiropractic manipulation, and dizziness when changing positions.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At 4:50 A.M. on August 5, Collins discharged the decedent and advised him to follow up with an orthopedic evaluation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At 7:57 A.M. on August 8, the decedent suffered cardiac arrest and died. The causes of death noted on his death certificate were septic shock, acute septic arthritis of the left shoulder, metabolic acidosis, and acute-on-chronic renal failure.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Mark Richman, a board-certified emergency and internal medicine physician, opined that Collins's care and treatment of the decedent fell below the accepted standard of care for a qualified nurse practitioner.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Mark Richman

evidence_index.text: Richman opined that Collins failed to recognize and appreciate signs and symptoms of possible septic arthritis.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Mark Richman

evidence_index.text: We hold that the decision of the tribunal was erroneous and accordingly vacate the judgment of dismissal.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: Richman's expert opinion, rooted in the evidence, was sufficient to meet the tribunal standard.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

evidence_index.text: The tribunal's apparent conclusion to the contrary is unsupported.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

evidence_index.text: The judgment of dismissal as to Collins is vacated, and the plaintiff may proceed with her claims against him without posting a bond.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient fell approximately two weeks prior, did not experience pain until one week afterward. Presented with inability to use left arm.

focused_exam.shoulder: Tenderness and limited range of motion in left shoulder

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Rotator cuff tendinitis and localized skin infection

requestables.name: Complete Blood Count
requestables.description: Blood work to assess for infection

reveal.result_summary: Elevated white blood cell count and neutrophils, low lymphocytes
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation for infection
Should have been ordered to confirm diagnosis

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with shoulder pain

key_events.event: Patient examined and diagnosed with rotator cuff tendinitis

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with shoulder pain and signs of infection
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Order CT scan of the shoulder
options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to rule out septic arthritis
Inadequate given signs of possible septic arthritis

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant discharged patient with oral antibiotics
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Attributed worsening pain to chiropractic manipulation

expected_action_court.description: CT scan should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for imaging

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failed to recognize signs of septic arthritis
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed septic shock and died

key_findings: Failure to order necessary imaging and admit for further treatment
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented death

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order CT imaging and admit for treatment
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Earlier diagnosis and treatment could have prevented deterioration

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents to ED with shoulder pain

tests_performed.result: Elevated WBC and neutrophils, low lymphocytes
